Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Christine Balboa Reed, Esq. of Low Ball & Lynch, T:831.655.8822, for defendants/moving party;
David L. Winnett, Esq. of The Veen Firm, PC, T:415.673.4800, for plaintiff/opposing party;
David L. Crowe, Esq. of Michel & Fackler, T:925.933.7309, for defenant Gil Construction;
Frances Yoshimura, Esq. of the Law Offices of John Biard, T:925.945.4491, for defendant JCW International, Inc.

Judge Kahn discloses that he considers plaintiff's counsel Elinor Leary a personal friend and has recommended her as an attorney to several people including to members of his family. However, Judge Kahn believes that he is fair and impartial in this case. No questions were asked or objections raised.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SH PROPERTY, INC. and THE BOHAN COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS is DENIED and their alternate MOTION FOR A TRIAL CONTINUANCE AND MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED. Plaintiff Danielle Paquette's unilateral cancellation of the defense mental examination less than a week prior to its long-scheduled date without providing documentation supporting the cancellation or agreeing to firm dates for the re-scheduling of the examination constitutes discovery abuse, especially considered in light of Ms. Paquette's prior efforts to resist the examination. Because the discovery abuse can be remedied by an award of monetary sanctions and continuance of the trial, it would be inequitable and contrary to California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits to grant the request for terminating sanctions. Ms. Paquette is required to pay $4,128 to the moving defendants no later than February 28, 2018. Ms. Paquette must make herself available for a defense mental examination consistent with the court's prior orders in this case on dates selected by the moving defendants no later than March 30, 2018. The trial is continued to November 5, 2018 at 9:30am in department 206.

Counsel for the moving defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.


Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants' SH Property, Inc. and The Bohan Company, Inc.'s motion for an order setting a mental health examination of plaintiff Danielle Paquette is denied without prejudice to being refiled if the motion papers comply with the requirements of CCP 2032.310(b). That section requires that the notice of motion must state the time, place, identity, specialty of the examiner and the "manner, conditions, scope and nature of the examination." The court rejects the apparent position of Ms. Paquette that there are no possible circumstances that can be shown by defendants that supports a court-ordered mental health examination of Ms. Paquette. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.
